pattern: For every pair of axis-aligned rectangles, compute their intersection region and find the largest square that fits inside it; return the maximum such square's area squared.
hints: Two axis-aligned rectangles intersect when max(a1.left, a2.left) < min(a1.right, a2.right) AND max(a1.bottom, a2.bottom) < min(a1.top, a2.top).
The intersection's width is min(r1x, r2x) - max(l1x, l2x) and height similarly; the largest square that fits has side = min(width, height).
Iterate all O(n²) rectangle pairs, compute each intersection's square side, and track the maximum; return side² (the area).

edge_inputs.description: two identical rectangles — entire region is intersection
rectangles share only an edge (zero-area intersection)
intersection is wider than tall — square limited by height
three rectangles where best square comes from pair 1&3, not 1&2 or 2&3

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    bottomLeft = []
    topRight = []
    for _ in range(n):
        x1 = rng.randint(1, 900)
        y1 = rng.randint(1, 900)
        x2 = rng.randint(x1 + 1, 1000)
        y2 = rng.randint(y1 + 1, 1000)
        bottomLeft.append([x1, y1])
        topRight.append([x2, y2])
    return [bottomLeft, topRight]
